description: This stacked bar chart quantifies the individual contributions of five distinct strengthening mechanisms to the total yield strength of Fe-26Mn-xAl-1C low-density steels across varying aluminum contents (3Al, 6Al, 10Al, and 12Al, corresponding to approximately 2.6–11.6 wt% Al). The y-axis displays yield strength in MPa, ranging from 0 to 1000, while each bar is segmented to represent Base strength, Solid solution by Al, Ferrite, Grain refining, and Carbide (κ-carbide) precipitation. The chart reveals a monotonic increase in total yield strength from approximately 320 MPa for 3Al to roughly 950 MPa for 12Al. While the 3Al and 6Al alloys derive strength primarily from base properties and solid solution hardening, the 12Al alloy exhibits a dramatic enhancement dominated by Carbide precipitation (approximately 360 MPa contribution), accompanied by significant contributions from Grain refining and Ferrite phases. This visualization substantiates the conclusion that κ-carbide precipitation becomes the predominant strengthening mechanism at higher aluminum contents, accounting for the exceptional yield strength in the 12Al composition compared to the modest gains from solid solution alone.
